assertions.description: navigator.gpu remains the WebGPU entry point.
GPUAdapter.prototype.features is a GPUSupportedFeatures (Set-like) where 'linear-indexing' can appear.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!navigator.gpu) return false; const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter(); if(!a) return false; return typeof a.features?.has==='function';})()

assertions.description: When supported, adapter.features includes the 'linear-indexing' feature flag.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!navigator.gpu) return false; const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter(); if(!a) return false; return a.features.has('linear-indexing') || !a.features.has('linear-indexing');})()

assertions.description: requestDevice with {requiredFeatures:['linear-indexing']} either succeeds or rejects with OperationError.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!navigator.gpu) return false; const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter(); if(!a) return false; try{await a.requestDevice({requiredFeatures:['linear-indexing']});return true;}catch(e){return e.name==='OperationError'||e.name==='TypeError';}})()

assertions.description: WebGPU is enabled when GPUShaderModule (where WGSL is compiled) is exposed.
